message:
fix(fnp-conformance): venv bootstrap reuses existing venv + --clear on partial state

All four conformance nditer smoke tests failed when the repo's
.venv-numpy314 directory existed on the rch worker from a prior run but
python3 wasn't inside — either because a previous bootstrap crashed
between uv venv and uv pip install, or because rch sync'd only the
directory skeleton. Previous code unconditionally ran 'uv venv ...'
which refuses to overwrite an existing directory, then 'uv pip install
--python .../bin/python3' which errors out because the venv is malformed.

Fix: classify the existing state.
- If python_path.is_file() — venv is fully bootstrapped, skip 'uv venv',
  reuse the existing interpreter and re-run 'uv pip install numpy' (idempotent).
- If venv_dir.exists() but python binary is missing — partial bootstrap.
  Pass --clear to 'uv venv' to force recreate before installing numpy.
- Otherwise — fresh 'uv venv'.

Applied to all three bootstrap sites: tests/smoke.rs,
src/ufunc_differential.rs, src/cross_engine_benchmark.rs.

Verified: workspace 'cargo test --workspace --all-targets --no-fail-fast'
now green (380 python + 2049 ufunc + 222 conformance + 6 smoke + rest).
